data.title: Brainstorm gap: monorepo multi-stack remediation behavior unspecified
data.description:
Epic w21-bsnz (remediate legacy code) scenario analysis identified that detect-stack.sh resolves a single stack based on marker-file priority. In a monorepo with both pyproject.toml and package.json (e.g., Flask API + Next.js frontend), remediate would select ruff as the linter and look up Python-specific confidence scores for JavaScript files.

The spec does not address whether:
- The skill accepts a --stack flag to override detection
- The skill scans for all detected stacks and runs each with appropriate engines
- The skill uses the primary detected stack only (current behavior) and documents the limitation

Impact: On any multi-stack nava-platform project, remediate would silently use wrong confidence scores for files outside the primary stack, causing patterns to be skipped or misrouted.

Resolution: Clarify user intent on multi-stack support scope and add to SC1 or SC2 before preplanning.
